violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to register an employee certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Per PIC, employee is signed and will be completing course and test within next week. Inspector will follow up and confirm registration. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment is to sanitize all dishes in the sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink until unit is repaired or replaced. Per PIC, establishment is having new dish machine installed tomorrow and will send evidence of installation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the dry storage 2-door reach-in. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to register an employee certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Raw animal foods (such as marinated chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as prepared sauces) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Raw food items were moved during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment is to sanitize all dishes in the sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink until unit is repaired or replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Tuna was observed thawed in its original intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP) in the 2-door reach-in. Seafood items packaged in ROP packaging shall be removed prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Food items were observed at 42-43 degrees Fahrenheit and were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the dry storage 2-door reach-in. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the main kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
